summary: Corrected swapped/misassigned GX TLUT symbol names. The function at 0x8033F024, previously still named func_8033F024, was identified as GXInitTlutObj, while the function at 0x8033F228, previously named GXInitTlutObj, was renamed to GXInitTlutRegion. Callsites and headers were updated accordingly across GX assembly and sysdolphin baselib assembly. The stated evidence was that both functions exactly match Zoids VS versions with debug symbols.
what_changed: Renamed the symbol at 0x8033F024 in asm/dolphin/gx/GXTexture.s from func_8033F024 to GXInitTlutObj.
Renamed the later TLUT-related symbol at 0x8033F228 in asm/dolphin/gx/GXTexture.s from GXInitTlutObj to GXInitTlutRegion.
Updated GX initialization callsites in asm/dolphin/gx/GXInit.s to call GXInitTlutRegion instead of GXInitTlutObj for TLUT region setup loops.
Updated baselib callsites in psdisp.s, sobjlib.s, and tobj.s to call GXInitTlutObj instead of the unnamed func_8033F024.
Updated src/dolphin/gx/GX_unknown_001.h prototype from func_8033F024(unk_t, u32, s32, u16) to GXInitTlutObj(unk_t, u32, s32, u16).
Updated src/dolphin/gx/__GX_unknown_001.h prototype at 0x0033BE08 from GXInitTlutObj(s32 *, s32, s32) to GXInitTlutRegion(s32 *, s32, s32).
smart_moves: Used external debug-symbol evidence from Zoids VS to resolve two very similar GX TLUT names rather than relying only on local callsite guesses.
Fixed both sides of the naming swap in one PR: the object initializer got the GXInitTlutObj name, and the region initializer got the GXInitTlutRegion name.
Propagated the symbol correction through assembly callsites and C header declarations, preventing mixed names across asm and src layers.
Preserved matching while improving semantic clarity; the diff only changes labels, branch targets, and extern declarations, not instruction bytes or behavior.
decomp_lessons: When two SDK-style functions have similar names and adjacent addresses, verify the exact symbol mapping before promoting an unknown function name.
External games with matching SDK code and debug symbols can be strong evidence for Dolphin/GX symbol recovery.
A naming correction should include all callsites and header prototypes, not just the defining label, or future decompilation will inherit stale names.

GXInitTlutObj and GXInitTlutRegion are distinct concepts: PR evidence maps the 4-argument TLUT object initializer at 0x8033F024 to GXInitTlutObj and the region initializer at 0x8033F228 to GXInitTlutRegion.
naming_conventions: Use official Dolphin SDK-style GX names with exact capitalization: GXInitTlutObj and GXInitTlutRegion.
Replace raw address-derived placeholder names like func_8033F024 once reliable symbol evidence exists.
Do not assign GXInitTlutObj to the TLUT region initializer; reserve GXInitTlutRegion for the region setup routine.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renaming assembly globals is sufficient when instruction bytes already match and only symbol identity is wrong.
Callsites that branch to the same address should be updated to the recovered semantic symbol name to keep asm searchable.
The PR notes exact matches with Zoids VS debug-symbol versions as the basis for symbol identification.
No new matching trick or codegen workaround is shown; this is primarily a symbol/header cleanup.
review_feedback: Human text stated: "They're both exact matches with the ones from Zoids VS which have debug symbols."
No line review comments were present in the provided context.
One review was recorded, but the provided excerpt does not include detailed reviewer feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace func_8033F06C with an official GX TLUT-related name.
Search for remaining callsites or prototypes using func_8033F024 to ensure the rename was complete.
Check whether GXInitTlutObj and GXInitTlutRegion later moved from unknown GX headers into official gx texture headers.
Search for other PRs citing Zoids VS debug symbols as evidence for Dolphin SDK symbol names.
